a.label: carrier O / gauge-normal form
a.standard_term: slice (normal form) for the linear gauge action g a^T + a g^T on symmetric matrices, with invariant quotient coordinates

b.label: parity law (normalization boundary)
b.standard_term: sign/normalization boundary condition governing the coupling-form invariant under parity of n (novel boundary-behavior clause of the depth theorem, not a classical named law)

evidence.label: Part VIII — Parity law
evidence.summary:
```
σ_even ∈ ℚ ,   σ_odd ∈ ℚ(√q)      (= Lovelock parity)
```
The even scalar curvature is rational; the mean-curvature / odd orders carry one factor `√q` (the unit normal). It is the `q^((r+2)/2)` normalization, not a d
